2548	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2549	[Basic-open restriction of a $\widetilde{N}$ sheaf is a module localization]
2550	  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
2551	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
2552	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Schemes" (tag 020J), \S 7, Lemma on the sheaves
2553	  % \widetilde M on Spec(R), item (4) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L691-L702).
2554	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2555	  % "For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$ as an $R_f$-module."
2556	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Schemes", \S 7, item (4); Spec-local core of
2557	  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}, part (2).}
2558	  Let \(N\) be an \(R\)-module and \(f \in R\). Then the presheaf restriction map of the
2559	  associated sheaf \(\widetilde{N}\) on \(\Spec R\) from the global sections
2560	  \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, \top)\) to the sections on the basic open \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f))\)
2561	  exhibits \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f))\) as the localized module
2562	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1} N\); that is, it is \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\)
2563	  over \(R\).
2564	\end{lemma}
2565	\begin{proof}
2566	  Mathlib supplies the localization-out-of-\(N\) instance
2567	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) for the canonical map
2568	  \(N \to \Gamma(\widetilde N, D(f))\). Since the global-sections map
2569	  \(N \to \Gamma(\widetilde N, \top)\) is an isomorphism and the canonical map to \(D(f)\) factors
2570	  through it followed by the restriction, precomposing the localization map with the inverse
2571	  isomorphism transfers the \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\) property to the restriction map
2572	  (Mathlib's transfer lemma for localization along a linear equivalence).
2573	\end{proof}

2575	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2576	[Basic-open restriction localizes for a sheaf in the essential image of $\widetilde{(-)}$]
2577	  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
2578	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
2579	  \uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
2580	  \textit{Affine engine of \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}: the basic-open
2581	  restriction localizes for any module sheaf that is \(\widetilde{N}\) up to isomorphism.}
2582	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) such that the counit
2583	  \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism (equivalently, \(M\) lies in the essential image of
2584	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\); Mathlib's \(\mathrm{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_iff}\)), and let \(f \in R\). Then the
2585	  presheaf restriction of \(M\) from \(\Gamma(M, \top)\) to \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\) is
2586	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).
2587	\end{lemma}
2588	\begin{proof}
2589	  The image of \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) under \(\mathrm{modulesSpecToSheaf}\) is an isomorphism;
2590	  forgetting to the presheaf gives a natural isomorphism \(\psi\) whose components are isomorphisms
2591	  of \(R\)-modules. Its naturality square for the inclusion \(D(f) \hookrightarrow \top\)
2592	  intertwines the restriction maps of \(\widetilde{N}\) (for \(N = \Gamma(M, \top)\)) and of \(M\).
2593	  Transporting \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict} across the two component isomorphisms
2594	  (pre- and post-composing by the linear equivalences induced by the components of \(\psi\))
2595	  identifies the restriction of \(M\) with a localization map, hence it is
2596	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\).

2758	  \(\mathrm{QuasicoherentData}\,M\) is nonempty. A datum

2847	\begin{lemma}
2848	
2849	\leanok
2850	[Finite basic-open cover refining a quasi-coherence cover]
2851	  \label{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}
2852	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}
2853	  \uses{lem:isQuasicoherent_quasicoherentData_mathlib, lem:isBasis_basic_opens_mathlib}
2854	  \textit{The topological finite-cover front of the gap1 transport: any quasi-coherence cover refines
2855	  to a finite basic-open cover.}
2856	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) and let \(q\) be quasi-coherence data for \(M\)
2857	  (\cref{lem:isQuasicoherent_quasicoherentData_mathlib}): a — possibly infinite — open cover
2858	  \(\{q.X_i\}\) of \(\top\) together with a presentation of \(M.\mathrm{over}\,(q.X_i)\) on each
2859	  member. Then there is a \emph{finite} subset \(t \subseteq R\) whose basic opens cover \(\Spec R\)
2860	  (equivalently \(\mathrm{span}\,t = (1)\)) such that each \(D(r)\), \(r \in t\), is contained in some
2861	  member \(q.X_i\) of the presentation cover.
2862	\end{lemma}
2863	\begin{proof}
2864	  \uses{lem:isBasis_basic_opens_mathlib}
2865	  The set \(G = \{\,r \in R : D(r) \le q.X_i \text{ for some } i\,\}\) spans the unit ideal. Indeed
2866	  every prime of \(R\) lies in some member \(q.X_i\) by the covering property \(q.\mathrm{coversTop}\)
2867	  (read off the \(\mathrm{Opens}\) Grothendieck-topology sieve), and the basic opens form a basis of
2868	  the Zariski topology (\cref{lem:isBasis_basic_opens_mathlib}), so a basic open \(D(r) \subseteq
2869	  q.X_i\) is a neighbourhood of that prime; hence \(\bigsqcup_{r \in G} D(r) = \top\), which is to say
2870	  \(\mathrm{span}\,G = (1)\). A unit-ideal membership uses only finitely many of the generators,
2871	  yielding a finite \(t \subseteq G\) with \(\mathrm{span}\,t = (1)\) and each \(D(r) \le q.X_i\) for
2872	  \(r \in t\).
2873	\end{proof}

3137	\end{lemma}
3138	\begin{proof}
3139	  \uses{def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr, lem:overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous,
3140	    lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous, lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib,
3141	    lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib, lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib,
3142	    lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
3143	  The isomorphism is built in four steps, ascending from the purely topological layer to the
3144	  module-level statement.
3145	
3146	  \emph{Step 1 (topological layer --- done).} The opens functor of the open immersion
3147	  \(U.\iota\) realizes the slice of the opens site of \(X\) over \(U\) as the opens site of the
3148	  subspace \(U\); on sheaf categories this is the equivalence
3149	  \(\mathrm{Sheaf}(J_X|_U,\,A) \simeq \mathrm{Sheaf}(J_U,\,A)\) of
3150	  \cref{def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr}, for every target category \(A\). This is the
3151	  topos-theoretic layer, assembled from the cocontinuity/continuity of both legs of
3152	  \(\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,U\) (\cref{lem:opens_overEquivalence_mathlib}).
3153	
3154	  \emph{Step 2 (geometric ring-sheaf identification --- the current obstacle).} Identify the
3155	  sliced structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X.\mathrm{over}\,U\) with the structure sheaf
3156	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U}\) of the open subscheme \(U.\mathrm{toScheme}\), transported across the
3157	  \(\mathrm{RingCat}\)-valued instance of \cref{def:overEquivalence_sheafCongr}; this is an
3158	  isomorphism of sheaves of rings. The bridge is the factorization of the opens functor of the
3159	  immersion,
3160	  \[
3161	    U.\iota.\mathrm{opensFunctor}
3162	      \;=\; (\mathrm{Opens.overEquivalence}\,U).\mathrm{inverse}
3163	        \;\circ\; \mathrm{Over.forget}\,U ,
3164	  \]
3165	  under which the structure-sheaf comparison reduces to the standard open-immersion
3166	  structure-sheaf identifications; recall that \(\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,U.\iota\)
3167	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib}) is precisely pushforward along
3168	  \(U.\iota.\mathrm{opensFunctor}\). This ring-sheaf identification is the remaining geometric
3169	  obstacle.
3170	
3171	  \emph{Step 3 (lift to modules).} Given the step-2 identification of the underlying ring sheaves
3172	  and the continuity of both legs (\cref{lem:overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous},
3173	  \cref{lem:overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous}), lift the sheaf-of-rings comparison to an
3174	  equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules via
3175	  \(\mathrm{pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence}\)
3176	  (\cref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}). Under this equivalence the sliced
3177	  module \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) corresponds to the geometric restriction
3178	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,U.\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\).
3179	
3180	  \emph{Step 4 (compose).} Compose with
3181	  \(\mathrm{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\) (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib})
3182	  to land the isomorphism \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U \cong M.\mathrm{restrict}\,U.\iota \cong
3183	  U.\iota^{*}M\). The two sides live a priori in different module categories, so the literal Lean
3184	  statement of \(\mathrm{overRestrictIso}\) is phrased through the step-3 equivalence functor; for
3185	  \(X = \Spec R\) and \(U = D(r)\) the target is \((\Spec R_r).\mathrm{Modules}\) under
3186	  \(D(r) \cong \Spec R_r\) (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}).
3187	\end{proof}

3191	\begin{lemma}
3192	
3193	\leanok
3194	[The slice-to-geometric isomorphism in pullback form (gap1, C, step 4')]
3195	  \label{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}
3196	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPullbackIso}
3197	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib,
3198	    lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}
3199	  \textit{The pullback packaging of the slice-touching bridge; the form consumed by the P1 transport.}
3200	  With \(X\) and \(U \subseteq X\) as above and \(M\) a sheaf of modules on \(X\), the transport of the
3201	  abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) under the step-3 equivalence functor
3202	  (\cref{def:over_restrict_equiv}) is canonically isomorphic to the inverse-image (pullback) of \(M\)
3203	  along the open immersion \(U.\iota\):
3204	  \[
3205	    (\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,(M.\mathrm{over}\,U)
3206	      \;\cong\; (U.\iota^{*})\,M
3207	  \]
3208	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}). This is the pullback packaging of the slice-touching bridge
3209	  \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso}; it is exactly the form in which a presentation of
3210	  \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) is transported into a presentation of the geometric pullback \(U.\iota^{*}M\),
3211	  and it is the ingredient consumed by the per-element local-tilde transport
3212	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}.
3213	\end{lemma}
3214	\begin{proof}
3215	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}
3216	  Compose the slice-to-geometric isomorphism \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso}, which identifies the
3217	  transported slice restriction with the geometric restriction
3218	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,U.\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\), with the component at \(M\) of the natural
3219	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,U.\iota\)
3220	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}) identifying the geometric restriction with
3221	  the pullback \(U.\iota^{*}M\).
3222	\end{proof}

3229	\begin{definition}\leanok
3230	[Step-3 equivalence sends the unit module to the unit module]
3231	  \label{def:over_restrict_unit_iso}
3232	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictUnitIso}
3233	  \uses{def:over_restrict_equiv}
3234	  \textit{The step-3 slice equivalence functor preserves the structure-sheaf (unit) module.}
3235	  Let \(U \subseteq X\) be an open subscheme. The functor of the step-3 slice
3236	  equivalence (\cref{def:over_restrict_equiv}) carries the unit
3237	  (structure-sheaf) module \(\mathbf{1}\) of the over-site
3238	  \(X.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}.\mathrm{over}\,U\) to the unit module of
3239	  \(U.\mathrm{toScheme}.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}\):
3240	  \[
3241	    (\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,
3242	      \bigl(\mathbf{1}\,(X.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}.\mathrm{over}\,U)\bigr)
3243	      \;\cong\; \mathbf{1}\,(U.\mathrm{toScheme}.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}) .
3244	  \]
3245	\end{definition}
3246	\begin{proof}
3247	  \uses{def:over_restrict_equiv}
3248	  By construction the functor of the equivalence is the pushforward
3249	  \(\mathrm{pushforward}\,\psi_0\) of sheaves of modules along the
3250	  over-site/open-site comparison, where \(\psi_0 = \mathrm{homMk}(\mathbf{1})\)
3251	  is the identity ring comparison. The canonical unit comparison
3252	  \(\mathrm{unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\,\psi_0 : \mathbf{1}_S \to
3253	  (\mathrm{pushforward}\,\psi_0).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf{1}_R\) is an isomorphism
3254	  whenever the underlying ring comparison \(\psi_0\) is one: reflecting through the
3255	  forgetful passage to presheaves (\(\mathrm{toSheaf}/\mathrm{sheafToPresheaf}\)),
3256	  its component over each object \(V\) is the functor-image of
3257	  \(\psi_0.\mathrm{app}\,V\), hence an isomorphism (this \(\mathrm{IsIso}\,\psi\)
3258	  driven criterion is supplied by the auxiliary
3259	  \(\mathrm{isIso\_unitToPushforwardObjUnit\_of\_isIso}'\)). Here
3260	  \(\psi_0 = \mathrm{homMk}(\mathbf{1})\) is the identity, so the unit comparison
3261	  is an isomorphism, and its inverse is the asserted isomorphism. (Mathlib's
3262	  isomorphism statement for \(\mathrm{unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\) instead requires
3263	  a finality hypothesis on the comparison functor; the \(\mathrm{IsIso}\,\psi\)
3264	  driven form used here is the one the slice equivalence needs.)
3265	\end{proof}

3267	\begin{definition}\leanok
3268	[Transport of a slice presentation to a geometric presentation]
3269	  \label{def:over_restrict_presentation}
3270	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPresentation}
3271	  \uses{def:over_restrict_unit_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib}
3272	  \textit{A slice presentation yields a presentation of the geometric pullback.}
3273	  Let \(U \subseteq X\) be open and \(M\) a sheaf of modules on \(X\). A
3274	  presentation of the abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\)
3275	  yields a presentation of the geometric pullback \((U.\iota^{*})\,M\):
3276	  \[
3277	    (M.\mathrm{over}\,U).\mathrm{Presentation}
3278	      \;\longrightarrow\;
3279	    \bigl((U.\iota^{*})\,M\bigr).\mathrm{Presentation} .
3280	  \]
3281	\end{definition}
3282	\begin{proof}
3283	  \uses{def:over_restrict_unit_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib}
3284	  Apply the functorial transport of presentations \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\)
3285	  (\cref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}) along the step-3 slice equivalence functor
3286	  \((\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}\), using
3287	  \cref{def:over_restrict_unit_iso} to identify the image of the structure-sheaf
3288	  (unit) module with the target unit module; this lands a presentation of the
3289	  transported slice module
3290	  \((\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,(M.\mathrm{over}\,U)\).
3291	  Finally transport across the pullback packaging isomorphism
3292	  \((\mathrm{overRestrictEquiv}\,U).\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\,(M.\mathrm{over}\,U)
3293	  \cong (U.\iota^{*})\,M\) (\cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso}) via
3294	  \(\mathrm{Presentation.ofIsIso}\), giving a presentation of the geometric
3295	  pullback \((U.\iota^{*})\,M\).
3296	\end{proof}

3373	\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent from a local-tilde cover (gap1 keystone, D)]
3374	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent}
3375	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent}
3376	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3377	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3378	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
3379	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3380	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent does NOT yet exist; it is the
3381	  % genuine new content of gap1 and is reused by gap2's section-localization characterization.
3382	  % isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation does NOT close it: that lemma needs a GLOBAL
3383	  % presentation of M, whereas here only per-D(r) tilde data is available.
3384	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S "Sections over principal opens",
3385	  % Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2152-L2170).
3386	  % Cf. [Hartshorne], II.5.3.
3387	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
3388	  % "Let $X$ be a scheme. Let $f \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$.
3389	  %  Denote $X_f \subset X$ the open where $f$ is invertible, see
3390	  %  Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-f-open}.
3391	  %  If $X$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated, the canonical map
3392	  %  $$
3393	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{O}_X)
3394	  %  $$
3395	  %  is an isomorphism. Moreover, if $\mathcal{F}$ is a quasi-coherent
3396	  %  sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules the map
3397	  %  $$
3398	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})
3399	  %  $$
3400	  %  is an isomorphism."
3401	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S ``Sections over principal opens'',
3402	  Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections}; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.3.}
3403	  Let \(M\) be a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) (equivalently, by
3404	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent} and
3405	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}, \(M\) is locally isomorphic to a
3406	  tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) on the members of a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) of
3407	  \(\Spec R\)). Then for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction
3408	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the localized module
3409	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top)\); that is, it is
3410	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).
3411	\end{lemma}
3412	% NOTE: the lemma STATEMENT is cited to Stacks lemma-invert-f-sections / Hartshorne II.5.3; the proof
3413	% below is a project-internal descent (finite sheaf equalizer over the cover + flatness of
3414	% localization) that deliberately does NOT route through the global affine equivalence (which is
3415	% gap1 itself), so there is no transcribed `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF`.
3416	\begin{proof}
3417	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3418	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3419	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
3420	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3421	  Refine the quasi-coherence cover to a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}_{j=1}^m\) of \(\Spec R\)
3422	  with each \(D(r_j) \le q.X_{i(j)}\)
3423	  (\cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). On each \(D(r_j)\) the restricted
3424	  counit is an isomorphism (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}), so
3425	  \(M|_{D(r_j)} \cong \widetilde{N_j}\) with \(N_j = \Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) a module over \(R_{r_j}\),
3426	  and the basic-open sections of \(M\) below \(D(r_j)\) are the corresponding module localizations
3427	  (\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}).
3428	
3429	  Fix \(f \in R\). The sheaf condition for \(M\) over the finite cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) and its pairwise
3430	  intersections presents the global sections as the finite equalizer
3431	  \[
3432	    \Gamma(M, \top) \;=\; \mathrm{eq}\Bigl(\textstyle\prod_j \Gamma(M, D(r_j))
3433	      \;\rightrightarrows\; \prod_{j,k} \Gamma(M, D(r_jr_k))\Bigr),
3434	  \]
3435	  the Mayer--Vietoris / unique-gluing description of sections
3436	  (\cref{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}, \cref{lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}); the same holds
3437	  with \(\top\) replaced by \(D(f)\) and the cover by \(\{D(fr_j)\}\). Localizing at
3438	  \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) is exact and commutes with this finite limit because localization is flat
3439	  (\cref{lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib}). On each cover piece the localization of
3440	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) away from \(f\) is \(\Gamma(M, D(fr_j)) = \Gamma(M|_{D(r_j)}, D(f))\), a
3441	  localization of the tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) computed by
3442	  \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}; the same on the overlaps. Hence the localized equalizer
3443	  computes \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\), and the comparison map
3444	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is an isomorphism: the section
3445	  restriction is \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\). This is the
3446	  \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})\) of Stacks
3447	  \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} on \(X = \Spec R\), \(X_f = D(f)\), obtained directly from
3448	  local-tilde data without appeal to the global affine equivalence.
3449	\end{proof}

3465	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
3466	  % be an affine scheme. Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. Then
3467	  % $\mathcal{F}$ is isomorphic to the sheaf associated to the $R$-module $\Gamma(X,
3468	  % \mathcal{F})$."
3469	  \textit{The affine equivalence QCoh\((\Spec R)\) \(\simeq\) Mod\((R)\): a quasi-coherent module
3470	  sheaf on \(\Spec R\) is \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M)}\) up to canonical isomorphism.}
3471	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) that is quasi-coherent
3472	  (\(\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,M\)). Then the counit \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism;
3473	  equivalently \(M\) lies in the essential image of \(\widetilde{(-)} : \mathrm{Mod}(R) \to (\Spec
3474	  R).\mathrm{Modules}\).
3475	\end{lemma}
3476	% NOTE: the proof is a one-line assembly — feed the keystone descent (D) into the in-file
3477	% equivalence isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict — so it routes through no transcribed
3478	% Stacks gluing proof; the SOURCE QUOTE above cites the lemma STATEMENT (Stacks
3479	% lemma-quasi-coherent-affine).
3480	\begin{proof}
3481	  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent, lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3482	  Since \(M\) is quasi-coherent, the section-localization descent
3483	  (\cref{lem:section_localization_descent}) gives that for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction
3484	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over
3485	  \(R\). This is exactly the right-hand side of the equivalence
3486	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict} (already established in-file), whose
3487	  left-hand side is \(\mathrm{IsIso}\,M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\). Hence the counit is an isomorphism and
3488	  \(M\) is identified with \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M, \top)}\), placing \(M\) in the essential image of
3489	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\).
3490	\end{proof}

3492	\subsection{G1-assemble: from per-basic-open section localization to the
3493	\texorpdfstring{$\widetilde{(-)}$}{tilde}-counit}
3494	
3495	This subsection records the bridge \(\text{section-localization on every basic open} \Rightarrow
3496	\mathrm{IsIso}\,M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\): given that the section restriction localizes on every basic
3497	open, the tilde-Gamma counit is an isomorphism. Together with its converse
3498	\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ} it assembles the equivalence
3499	\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}, which is exactly what makes G1-core
3500	(\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}) and gap1
3501	(\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}) interderivable -- the route by which G1-core is obtained as
3502	a corollary of gap1. The supporting Mathlib facts are recorded as dependency anchors.
3503

3552	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3553	[A map intertwining two localizations is bijective]
3554	  \label{lem:bijective_comp_of_localizations}
3555	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.bijective_comp_of_localizations}
3556	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl is `private` in the source (a project-internal helper for the
3557	  % G1-assemble step); the \lean{} pin is recorded for the DAG but resolves only project-internally.
3558	  \uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_linearEquiv_mathlib, lem:isLocalizedModule_linearMap_ext_mathlib}
3559	  \textit{Linear-algebra glue for the G1-assemble step.}
3560	  Let \(S \subseteq R\) be a submonoid and let \(g : M \to M'\), \(g' : M \to M''\) both exhibit a
3561	  localization of \(M\) at \(S\). If a linear map \(h : M' \to M''\) satisfies \(h \circ g = g'\),
3562	  then \(h\) is bijective.
3563	\end{lemma}
3564	\begin{proof}
3565	  \uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_linearEquiv_mathlib, lem:isLocalizedModule_linearMap_ext_mathlib}
3566	  The map \(h\) agrees with the canonical localization isomorphism
3567	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv}\,S\,g\,g'\)
3568	  (\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_linearEquiv_mathlib}): both compose with \(g\) to give \(g'\), so they
3569	  coincide by uniqueness of maps out of a localization
3570	  (\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_linearMap_ext_mathlib}). A linear equivalence is bijective, hence so
3571	  is \(h\).
3572	\end{proof}

3574	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3575	[Basis-wise isomorphism of sheaves on $\Spec R$]
3576	  \label{lem:isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen}
3577	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen}
3578	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl is `private` in the source (a project-internal helper for the

3580	  \uses{lem:isBasis_basic_opens_mathlib, lem:stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis_mathlib,
3581	    lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}
3582	  \textit{Upgrades a per-basic-open isomorphism to an isomorphism of sheaves.}
3583	  Let \(\alpha : F \to G\) be a morphism of sheaves of \(R\)-modules on \(\Spec R\) whose component
3584	  on every basic open \(D(f)\) is an isomorphism. Then \(\alpha\) is an isomorphism.
3585	\end{lemma}
3586	\begin{proof}
3587	  \uses{lem:isBasis_basic_opens_mathlib, lem:stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis_mathlib,
3588	    lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}

3590	  of every component \(\alpha_{D(f)}\) gives, in particular, injectivity on each basis open, hence
3591	  injectivity on every stalk (\cref{lem:stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis_mathlib});
3592	  surjectivity on stalks follows by lifting germs through the bijective basic-open components. A

3597	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3598	[$\mathrm{IsIso}\,M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}$ from per-basic-open section localization
3599	(G1-assemble)]
3600	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3601	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3602	  \uses{lem:isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen, lem:bijective_comp_of_localizations,
3603	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3604	  \textit{The G1-assemble reduction: section-localization on every basic open forces the
3605	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-counit to be an isomorphism.}
3606	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\), and suppose that for every \(f \in R\) the section
3607	  restriction \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as
3608	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\) -- exactly the conclusion of G1-core
3609	  (\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}). Then the counit \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an
3610	  isomorphism.
3611	\end{lemma}
3612	\begin{proof}
3613	  \uses{lem:isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen, lem:bijective_comp_of_localizations,
3614	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3615	  Write \(N = \Gamma(M, \top)\). On each basic open \(D(f)\), the component of
3616	  \(\mathrm{modulesSpecToSheaf}(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ})\) is a map between two localizations of \(N\)
3617	  at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\): the source \(\Gamma(\widetilde N, D(f))\) localizes \(N\) via the affine
3618	  tilde map (\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}) and the target \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\)
3619	  localizes \(N\) by hypothesis, the two being intertwined by the compatibility
3620	  \(\mathrm{toOpen\_fromTildeΓ\_app}\). Hence the component is bijective
3621	  (\cref{lem:bijective_comp_of_localizations}), so an isomorphism. By
3622	  \cref{lem:isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen} the sheaf morphism
3623	  \(\mathrm{modulesSpecToSheaf}(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ})\) is then an isomorphism, and since
3624	  \(\mathrm{modulesSpecToSheaf}\) is fully faithful it reflects isomorphisms, giving
3625	  \(\mathrm{IsIso}\,M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\).
3626	\end{proof}

3628	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3629	[$\mathrm{IsIso}\,M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}$ characterized by section localization]
3630	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}
3631	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict}

3643	  The forward direction is \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ} (the affine
3644	  engine, applied for each \(f\)); the reverse direction is
3645	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict}.

3677	\begin{definition}\leanok

3682	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\). The
3683	  \emph{schematic-support immersion} is the canonical closed immersion
3684	  \[

3686	  \]
3687	  of the schematic support (\cref{def:schematic_support}) into the ambient
3688	  scheme, namely the subscheme immersion of the annihilator ideal sheaf

3690	  quasi-compact preimmersion onto the support, and it is the morphism whose
3691	  composite with a base morphism \(f : X \to S\) defines proper support
3692	  (\cref{def:has_proper_support}). Proved directly in Lean.

3695	\begin{lemma}[Properness as a morphism property]
3696	  \label{lem:isProper_mathlib}
3697	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper}
3698	  \mathlibok
3699	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
3700	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper}).}
3701	  A morphism \(f : X \to S\) of schemes is \emph{proper} iff it is separated,
3702	  universally closed, and locally of finite type. Properness is stable under base
3703	  change: if \(f : X \to S\) is proper and \(g : T \to S\) is any morphism then
3704	  the base change \(X \times_S T \to T\) is proper. Consequently the base-change
3705	  clause that Nitsure invokes (``properness \ldots\ preserved by base-change'')
3706	  applies directly to the proper-support condition below.
3707	\end{lemma}

3719	  % + L418-L419).

3722	  %  such that the schematic support of $\F$ is proper over $T$
3723	  %  and $\F$ is flat over $T$, together with

3766	\section{The Quot functor}
3767	\label{sec:quot_functor}
3768	
3769	\begin{definition}\leanok
3770	[Quot functor]
3771	  \label{def:quot_functor}
3772	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor}
3773	  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, def:has_proper_support}
3774	
3775	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "The Functors \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) and
3776	  % \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\)" (read from
3777	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L394-L433
3778	  % + L481-L498).
3779	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
3780	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(X\to S\) be a finite type
3781	  %  scheme over it. Let \(E\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X\).
3782	  %  Let \(Sch_S\) denote the category of all locally noetherian schemes
3783	  %  over \(S\). For any \(T\to S\) in \(Sch_S\), a \textbf{family of quotients of \(E\)
3784	  %  parametrised by \(T\)} will mean
3785	  %  a pair \((\F,q)\) consisting of
3786	  %
3787	  %  (i) a coherent sheaf \(\F\) on \(X_T = X\times_ST\)
3788	  %  such that the schematic support of \(\F\) is proper over \(T\)
3789	  %  and \(\F\) is flat over \(T\), together with
3790	  %
3791	  %  (ii) a surjective \({\mathcal O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism of sheaves
3792	  %  \(q:E_T  \to \F\) where \(E_T\) is the pull-back of \(E\) under
3793	  %  the projection \(X_T \to X\).
3794	  %
3795	  %  Two such families \((\F,q)\) and \((\F,q)\) parametrised by \(T\) will
3796	  %  be regarded as equivalent if \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\)),
3797	  %  and \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) will denote
3798	  %  an equivalence class. Then as properness and flatness are preserved
3799	  %  by base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact,
3800	  %  the pull-back of \(\langle \F, q \rangle\) under an \(S\)-morphism \(T'\to T\) is
3801	  %  well-defined, which gives
3802	  %  a set-valued contravariant functor
3803	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S} : Sch_S \to Sets\) under which
3804	  %  \(\)T\mapsto  \{\mbox{ All } \langle \F, q \rangle \mbox{ parametrised by }T\}\(\)
3805	  %  [...]
3806	  %  This shows that the functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\) naturally decomposes
3807	  %  as a co-product
3808	  %  \(\)\Quot_{E/X/S} = \coprod_{{\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]}\, \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\(\)
3809	  %  where for any polynomial \({\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]\), the functor
3810	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) associates
3811	  %  to any \(T\) the set of all equivalence classes of
3812	  %  families \(\langle \F,q \rangle\) such that at each \(t\in T\) the
3813	  %  Hilbert polynomial of the restriction \(\F_t\), calculated
3814	  %  using the pull-back of \(L\), is \({\Phi}\)."
3815	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
3816	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
3817	  \(L\) a line bundle on \(X\), \(E\) a coherent sheaf on \(X\), and
3818	  \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). The \emph{Quot functor with Hilbert
3819	  polynomial \(\Phi\)}
3820	  \[
3821	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set}
3822	  \]
3823	  sends an \(S\)-scheme \(T \to S\) to the set of equivalence classes
3824	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) where
3825	  \begin{enumerate}
3826	    \item \(\mathcal{F}\) is a coherent sheaf on \(X_T = X \times_S T\) whose
3827	      schematic support is proper over \(T\) (\cref{def:has_proper_support}) and
3828	      which is flat over \(T\);
3829	    \item \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is a surjective
3830	      \(\mathcal{O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism, where \(E_T\) is the pull-back
3831	      of \(E\) along \(X_T \to X\);
3832	    \item for every \(t \in T\) the Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}\)
3833	      with respect to \(L|_{X_t}\) (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
3834	      \(\Phi\).
3835	  \end{enumerate}
